title: WebSockets
description: Managed WebSocket endpoints with shared middleware and optional port isolation

blocks.text: app.ws() registers a WebSocket endpoint. Howl runs the middleware pipeline, performs the upgrade, and wires your handlers as listeners. Each handler gets the socket and the request Context, so middleware-set state (auth, request id) is available before the first frame.
A non-WebSocket request to a registered WS path returns 426 Upgrade Required.

rows: A protocol or transport error occurs.

blocks.text: All four are optional. ctx is the same context the middleware chain built, so middleware auth applies to the socket exactly as it does to HTTP routes.
Run your normal auth middleware before app.ws(); it populates ctx.state, then reject unauthenticated upgrades in open() with a close code.

Pass options.port to bind a WS endpoint to its own Deno.serve listener. app.listen() spawns it automatically, sharing the middleware pipeline but exposing only that port's WS paths — everything else 404s there, and the path is hidden from the main port.
Use port isolation when the WebSocket surface should scale or terminate TLS independently from the rest of the HTTP app.
